methods.description: Process for preparing and analyzing flower buds for meiotic studies, including fixation, slide preparation, and cell examination to calculate the meiotic index.

steps.description: Flower buds were fixed in ethanol-acetic acid (3:1) at room temperature for 24 h, transferred to 70% alcohol and stored under refrigeration until use.
steps.searchable_summary: fix flower buds in ethanol-acetic acid and store in alcohol

steps.description: Temporary slides were prepared by the squashing technique and the cells were stained with 1% acetic carmine.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides using squashing technique and stain cells

steps.description: At least 25 cells were examined at each meiotic phase. The numbers of monads, dyads, triads, tetrads and polyads were recorded for calculation of the meiotic index.
steps.searchable_summary: examine cells and record meiotic products for index calculation

steps.description: The meiotic index considered in this study was the mean of four randomly sampled slides.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean meiotic index from sampled slides

methods.description: Method to assess pollen viability using staining techniques and microscopic examination to distinguish viable from sterile pollen grains.

steps.description: Fixed pollen grains of flowers in anthesis were stained with Alexander solution for wall/cytoplasm reactivity.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen grains with Alexander solution

steps.description: The anthers were squashed in a drop of stain and, after two minutes at room temperature, the pollen grains were checked, counted and measured with the aid of an eyepiece micrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: squash anthers and measure pollen grains with micrometer

steps.description: The number of viable and sterile pollen grains considered in this study was the mean of the count of these cells on five randomly sampled slides.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean viability from sampled slides

methods.description: Use of an optical microscope for observing and photographing samples during the study.

steps.description: An optical microscope was used for the observations and photographs.
steps.searchable_summary: use optical microscope for sample observation and photography

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data using the Genes program.

steps.description: The statistical analysis was performed using the Genes program.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis with Genes program
